evals.prompt: Do a full codebase audit on the repo at /Users/ericwellnitz/dev/ai-skills. I want the whole report — structure, stack, AI tools, dependencies, git history, quality, security, docs, architecture, infrastructure, licensing, API surface, team maturity, everything.
evals.expected_output: A complete codebase-diligence-report.md written to the ai-skills repo root. The report contains all sections including the executive scorecard with anchor links, AI Tools & LLM Usage as the first detail section, and all 11 scorecard categories.
expectations: The report file codebase-diligence-report.md is written to the repo root
The report header shows 'Depth: Standard'
The Executive Summary contains a Scorecard table with 11 categories, each with a Details anchor link
The Executive Summary contains a Top Risks section with severity-tagged items

The AI Tools & LLM Usage section appears before other detail sections and includes the AI Maturity Scorecard table with 4 dimensions (Adoption Level, Dependency Risk, Code Provenance Clarity, Governance Maturity)
The AI Tools section detects .claude/ directory and CLAUDE.md in this repo
All detail sections contain a severity header line (e.g., '🔴 N · 🟡 N · 🟢 N')
The report contains Security Posture, Architecture & Complexity, Infrastructure & Deployment, Licensing & Compliance, API Surface & Integrations, and Team & Process Maturity sections
The Findings Summary lists all 🔴 and 🟡 findings with section attribution
The Recommendations section contains items tagged [CRITICAL], [IMPORTANT], or [NICE TO HAVE]

evals.prompt: Give me a quick scan of the ai-skills repo. Just a fast overview.
evals.expected_output: A compact Quick Scan report written to the repo root. The report uses the 'At a Glance' table format, includes 5-7 key observations, and a recommendation about whether deeper analysis is warranted.
expectations: The report header shows 'Depth: Quick Scan'
The report contains an 'At a Glance' table with rows for Project Type, Tech Stack, Age/Activity, Contributors, AI Tool Adoption, Dependencies, and Secrets Hygiene
The report contains a Key Observations section with 5-7 severity-tagged bullets
The report contains a Recommendation section suggesting whether deeper analysis is needed
The report does NOT contain full detail sections like Architecture & Complexity or Licensing & Compliance
The report is substantially shorter than a Standard analysis

evals.prompt: I need a thorough due diligence on the ai-skills repo. We're evaluating it for acquisition. Deep audit please.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive Deep Audit report. The orchestrator spawns 3 specialist agents (ai-tools-analyst, security-analyst, infra-compliance-analyst) and creates artifacts in .diligence-report/. The final report includes Cross-Cutting Findings.
expectations: The report header shows 'Depth: Deep Audit'
A .diligence-report/ directory is created with context.md, ai-tools.md, security.md, and infra-compliance.md artifacts
The AI Tools & LLM Usage section contains deeper analysis than Standard (e.g., AI co-author temporal trends, LLM usage tracing, governance deep dive)
The Security Posture section contains deeper analysis (auth flow tracing, injection pattern analysis, security header checks)
The report contains a Cross-Cutting Findings section identifying patterns across domains
The Executive Summary Scorecard has all 11 categories with anchor links

evals.prompt: Focus on AI tools usage in the ai-skills repo. I want to know what AI tools are being used, how much code is AI-generated, and what the governance looks like.
evals.expected_output: A focused report containing only the Executive Summary, AI Tools & LLM Usage section, and Recommendations. Other sections are omitted.
expectations: The report contains an AI Tools & LLM Usage section with the AI Maturity Scorecard
The report detects .claude/ directory and CLAUDE.md as AI tool configurations
The report includes AI co-author commit analysis from git history
The report does NOT contain Dependency Health, Documentation Assessment, or Infrastructure sections
The report contains an Executive Summary and Recommendations section

evals.prompt: I just inherited a repo at ~/dev/mystery-project. Can you do a diligence report? I want to understand the state of it.
evals.expected_output: A Standard report written to the target repo. Missing elements are explicitly flagged as absent. Graceful degradation for any missing data.
expectations: The report is written to the target repo directory, not the current working directory
Absent elements (missing lock file, missing README, missing tests, etc.) are reported as ❌ Absent rather than silently omitted
The Findings Summary contains at least one 🔴 or 🟡 finding
Recommendations name specific files or actions, not vague advice
The Executive Summary mentions the most significant risk found
The AI Tools section reports findings (even if 'No AI tools detected')

evals.prompt: Deep audit on the ai-skills repo but only focus on security. I want a thorough security review.
evals.expected_output: A Deep Audit focused on security. Only the security-analyst agent is spawned. The report contains Executive Summary, Security Posture (deep), and Recommendations.
expectations: The security-analyst agent is spawned and writes .diligence-report/security.md
The report contains a detailed Security Posture section with subsections for secrets hygiene, authentication, input validation, security headers, and dependency security
The report does NOT contain AI Tools, Architecture, Infrastructure, or Team Maturity sections
The report contains Executive Summary and Recommendations
The ai-tools-analyst and infra-compliance-analyst agents are NOT spawned
